description: Digital proximity, ambient light, RGB, and gesture sensor, I2C

ALS/Color.name: ATIME
ALS/Color.description: ADC integration time register controls integration time for ALS/Color ADCs

ALS/Color.name: ATIME
quality_issues: identical field layout as adjacent register 0x81

fields.name: GEXTH
fields.description: Gesture Exit Threshold. This register sets the threshold value used to determine a “gesture end” and subsequent exit of the gesture state machine. Setting GTHR_OUT to 0x00 will prevent gesture exit until GMODE is set to 0.

fields.name: GFIFOTH
fields.description: Gesture FIFO Threshold. This value is compared with the FIFO Level (i.e. the number of UDLR datasets) to generate an interrupt (if enabled).

fields.name: GEXMSK
fields.description: Gesture Exit Mask. Controls which of the gesture detector photodiodes (UDLR) will be included to determine a “gesture end” and subsequent exit of the gesture state machine. Unmasked UDLR data will be compared with the value in GTHR_OUT. Field value bits correspond to UDLR detectors.

fields.name: GEXPERS
fields.description: Gesture Exit Persistence. When a number of consecutive “gesture end” occurrences become equal or greater to the GEPERS value, the Gesture state machine is exited.

Gesture.name: GCONF2

fields.name: GGAIN
fields.description: Gesture Gain Control. Sets the gain of the proximity receiver in gesture mode.

fields.name: GLDRIVE
fields.description: Gesture LED Drive Strength. Sets LED Drive Strength in gesture mode.

fields.name: GWTIME
fields.description: Gesture Wait Time. Controls the amount of time in a low power mode between gesture detection cycles.

quality_issues: all fields have default RW access (may be missing real access types)

Gesture.name: GCONF3
Gesture.description: Gesture Configuration Three Register contains settings that govern which gesture photodiode pair: UP-DOWN and/ or RIGHT-LEFT will be enabled

fields.name: GDIMS
fields.description: Gesture Dimension Select. Selects which gesture photodiode pairs are enabled to gather results during gesture.

Gesture.name: GCONF4
Gesture.description: Gesture Configuration Four Register contains settings that govern Gesture interrupts and interrupt clearing/reset as well as operation mode control and status

fields.name: GIEN
fields.description: Gesture interrupt enable. When asserted, all gesture related interrupts are unmasked.

fields.name: GMODE
fields.description: Gesture Mode. Reading this bit reports if the gesture state machine is actively running, 1 = Gesture, 0= ALS, Proximity, Color. Writing a 1 causes immediate entry into the gesture state machine; writing a 0 causes exit when conversion finished.

quality_issues: all fields have default RW access (may be missing real access types)

Gesture.name: GSTATUS
Gesture.description: Gesture Status Register indicates the operational condition of the gesture state machine

fields.name: GFOV
fields.description: Gesture FIFO Overflow. A setting of 1 indicates that the FIFO has filled to capacity and that new gesture detector data has been lost.

fields.name: GVALID
fields.description: Gesture FIFO Data. GVALID bit is sent when GFLVL becomes greater than GFIFOTH.

quality_issues: all fields have default RW access (may be missing real access types)

Interrupt.name: STATUS
Interrupt.description: Status register provides the status of the device

fields.name: PGSAT
fields.description: Analog saturation event during proximity or gesture cycle.

quality_issues: outlier field count (8 fields vs median 1)

fields.name: GEN
fields.description: Gesture Enable. When asserted, the gesture state machine can be activated. Activation is subject to the states of PEN and GMODEbits.

fields.name: PIEN
fields.description: Proximity Interrupt Enable. When asserted, it permits proximity interrupts to be generated, subject to the persistence filter settings.

fields.name: AIEN
fields.description: ALS Interrupt Enable. When asserted, it permits ALS interrupts to be generated, subject to the persistence filter settings.

fields.name: WEN
fields.description: Wait Enable. This bit activates the wait feature. Writing a one activates the wait timer. Writing a zero disables the wait timer.

fields.name: PEN
fields.description: Proximity Detect Enable. This field activates the proximity detection. Writing a one activates the proximity. Writing a zero disables the proximity.

fields.name: AEN
fields.description: ALS Enable. This field activates ALS function. Writing a one activates the ALS. Writing a zero disables the ALS.

fields.name: PON
fields.description: Power ON. This field activates the internal oscillator to permit the timers and ADC channels to operate. Writing a one activates the oscillator. Writing a zero disables the oscillator and puts the device into a low power sleep mode. During reads and writes over the I2C interface, this bit is temporarily overridden and the oscillator is enabled, independent of the state of PON.

fields.name: PPERS
fields.description: Proximity Interrupt Persistence. Controls rate of proximity interrupt to the host processor.

fields.name: APERS
fields.description: ALS Interrupt Persistence. Controls rate of Clear channel interrupt to the host processor.

fields.name: WLONG
fields.description: Wait Long.When asserted, the wait cycle is increased by a factor 12x from that programmed in theWTIME register.

fields.name: PSIEN
fields.description: Proximity Saturation Interrupt Enable. 0 = Proximity saturation interrupt disabled, 1 = Proximity saturation interrupt enabled

fields.name: CPSIEN
fields.description: Clear Photodiode Saturation Interrupt Enable. 0 = ALS Saturation Interrupt disabled, 1 = ALS Saturation Interrupt enabled

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved. Write as 0. Reserved. Write as 0.

fields.name: PCMP
fields.description: Proximity Gain Compensation Enable. This bit provides gain compensation when proximity photodiode signal is reduced as a result of sensor masking. If only one diode of the diode pair is contributing, then only half of the signal is available at the ADC; this results in a maximum ADC value of 127. Enabling PCMP enables an additional gain of 2X, resulting in a maximum ADC value of 255. Proximity Gain Compensation Enable. This bit provides gain compensation when proximity photodiode signal is reduced as a result of sensor masking. If only one diode of the diode pair is contributing, then only half of the signal is available at the ADC; this results in a maximum ADC value of 127. Enabling PCMP enables an additional gain of 2X, resulting in a maximum ADC value of 255.

fields.name: DATA
fields.description: 8-bit result from the left directional sensor
